description: Controlled category language registry. Canonical terms for evidence-risk intelligence discourse.

terms.definition: The observable condition of an evidence artifact or evidence chain before belief, action, publication, escalation, or institutional response. Not a truth verdict.
A discrete item of digital evidence: an image, video, audio recording, document, screenshot, source record, claim record, or media object subject to evidence posture classification.
The doctrine that Hoax.ai classifies evidence artifacts and evidence chains, not the people, institutions, brands, organizations, or events connected to them.
terms.notes: DEC-012 and GOVERNANCE_BOUNDARY.md Artifact–Subject Separation Doctrine.

terms.definition: The collapse of easy confidence in the evidence layer when synthetic media, altered sources, and simulated contexts can be produced at scale.
terms.notes: Strategic thesis framing. Conceptual, not operational detection claim.

terms.definition: A weakness, break, or uncertainty in the origin, custody, metadata, source chain, or traceability of an evidence artifact.
A bounded assessment of how reliably a source, publication path, or origin signal supports examination of an evidence artifact. Not a truth certification.
The degree to which an evidence artifact fits known temporal, geographic, institutional, and situational constraints without unresolved contextual conflict.
terms.notes: Context describes artifact fit, not event or subject verdict.

terms.definition: Structural, linguistic, media, temporal, and format-based coherence signals and uncertainty markers indicating whether an evidence artifact holds together under examination.
terms.notes: Coherence signals only. Not absolute detection.

terms.definition: A linked sequence of evidence artifacts, source records, and custody signals that together form an examinable evidence structure.
terms.notes: Chain classification applies to artifacts, not subjects.

terms.definition: The governed limit on what Hoax.ai outputs may state: evidence posture, risk signals, confidence, uncertainty, and recommended checks — never final truth verdicts or subject accusation.
terms.notes: Required for all future tool and protocol outputs.

terms.definition: A future factory layer — taxonomy, standard, protocol, classifier, or reference page — that is planned or under development but must not be described as existing or operational.
terms.notes: Maps to evidence_posture planned_not_claimed in evidence ledger.

terms.definition: Governed intelligence about the condition, uncertainty, and risk signals of evidence artifacts and evidence chains in the synthetic media age. Not truth detection or subject accusation.
